text: Occupation, Resistance and Liberation 1944/45 This book focuses on different aspects of the Soviet occupation of Austria, Hungary and Romania: political, military and economic. Based on a large amount of documentation in Austrian, Hungarian, Romanian and Russian archives, the authors contribute to a better understanding of the nature of Soviet foreign policy. The literature on the subject has so far focused on the political dimension, namely when and why Stalin decided to introduce the Bolshevik political system in the Soviet sphere of interest. In this respect the book falls into the category of studies dealing with the imperial dimension of the Soviet conquest of Eastern Central Europe and therefore with the use of occupied territory as military and economic space. Finally, it explores the traumatic human experience of Soviet occupation: war crimes, rape, looting and deportations.
headline: Fully Human Written
